---
title: "Untitled"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
# Library section
library(quantreg)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyverse)
library(sn)
library(lmtest)
library(sandwich)
```

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
set.seed(479)
sample_size <- c(50, 100, 200, 450, 700)
repli <- 1000
beta0_original <- 20
beta1_original <- 0.6
sigma_error <- 1.9
mean_error <- 0
beta_error <- 1/7
```

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
set.seed(479)
slope_func <- function(data, indices) {
  d <- data[indices, ]
  fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = d)
  coef(fit)[2]
}
```

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
library(sn)
set.seed(479)

skewness <- c(0, 2.5, 3, 4, 4.5, 5)

ols_qr_skew <- list()

for (skew in skewness) {
  for (i in seq_along(sample_size)) {
    n <- sample_size[i]
    skew_list <- list()
   
    for (j in 1:repli) {
      if (skew == 0) {
        error <- rnorm(n, mean = mean_error, sd = sigma_error)
      } else {
        shape <- 4 / skew^2
        rate <- sqrt(shape) / sigma_error
        error <- rgamma(n, shape = shape, rate = rate)
        error <- error - shape / rate   # center to mean 0
      }

      x <- runif(n, 0, 5)
      y <- beta0_original + beta1_original * x + error
      dat <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)
     
      ols <- lm(y ~ x, data = dat)
      slope_ols <- coef(ols)[2]
      
      se_slope_ols <- summary(ols)$coefficients["x", "Std. Error"]
      pval_ols <- summary(ols)$coefficients["x", "Pr(>|t|)"]
      ci_lower_ols <- slope_ols - 1.96 * se_slope_ols
      ci_upper_ols <- slope_ols + 1.96 * se_slope_ols
      
      bias <- slope_ols - beta1_original
      interval_length <- ci_upper_ols - ci_lower_ols
      
      absolute_percentage_bias <- abs(slope_ols - beta1_original) / beta1_original * 100
      
      skew_list[[length(skew_list) + 1]] <- tibble(
        Run = j,
        Sample_Size = n,
        Model = "OLS",
        Skewness = skew,
        Slope = round(slope_ols, 4),
        Bias = slope_ols - beta1_original,
        SE_Slope = round(se_slope_ols, 4),
        Coverage = (ci_lower_ols <= beta1_original) && (ci_upper_ols >= beta1_original),
        Power = pval_ols < 0.05,
        CI_Lower = round(ci_lower_ols, 4),
        CI_Upper = round(ci_upper_ols, 4),
        True_Slope = beta1_original
      )
      
        qr <- rq(y ~ x, tau = 0.5, data = dat)
        sum_qr <- summary(qr, se = "boot", bsmethod = "xy", R = 200)
        
        slope_qr <- coef(qr)[2]
        se_slope_qr  <- sum_qr$coefficients["x", "Std. Error"]
        p_qr <- sum_qr$coefficients["x", "Pr(>|t|)"] 
        ci_lower_qr <- slope_qr - 1.96 * se_slope_qr
        ci_upper_qr <- slope_qr + 1.96 * se_slope_qr
        
        skew_list[[length(skew_list) + 1]] <- tibble(
          Run = j,
          Sample_Size = n,
          Model = paste0("QR_0.5"),
          Skewness = skew, 
          Slope = round(slope_qr, 4),
          Bias = slope_qr - beta1_original,
          SE_Slope = round(se_slope_qr, 4),
          Coverage = (ci_lower_qr <= beta1_original) && (ci_upper_qr >= beta1_original),
          Power = p_qr < 0.05,
          CI_Lower = round(ci_lower_qr, 4),
          CI_Upper = round(ci_upper_qr, 4),
          True_Slope = beta1_original
        )
      
    }
      ols_qr_skew[[length(ols_qr_skew) + 1]] <- bind_rows(skew_list)
  }
}

ols_qr_skew <- bind_rows(ols_qr_skew)
head(ols_qr_skew, n = 1)
```

```{r}
bias_skew <- ggplot(ols_qr_skew, aes(x = factor(Sample_Size), y = Bias, fill = Model)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, outlier.alpha = 0.3) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.5) +
  facet_wrap(~ Skewness, scales = "free_y", ncol = 2) +
  labs(title = "Bias of Slope Estimates", x = "Sample Size", fill = "Model") +
  theme_minimal()
print(bias_skew)
saveRDS(bias_skew, "bias_skew.rds")
```

```{r}
slope_skew <- ggplot(ols_qr_skew, aes(x = factor(Sample_Size), y = Slope, fill = Model)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  facet_wrap(~ Skewness, scales = "free_y") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = beta1_original, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", 
           xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = 0.6 - 1.96*sqrt(0.6*0.95/repli),
           ymax = 0.6 + 1.96*sqrt(0.6*0.95/repli),
           alpha = 0.3, fill = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Slope Estimates", x = "Sample Size")
print(slope_skew)
saveRDS(slope_skew, "slope_skew.rds")
```

```{r}
# skew_qr <- ols_qr_skew %>% 
#   filter(Model == "QR_0.5") %>%
#   ggplot(aes(x = factor(Sample_Size), y = Slope)) +
#   geom_boxplot(fill = "#4ECDC4", alpha = 0.7) +
#   facet_wrap(~ Skewness, scales = "free_y") +
#   geom_hline(yintercept = beta1_original, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
#   annotate("rect", 
#            xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
#            ymin = 0.6 - 1.96*sqrt(0.6*0.95/repli),
#            ymax = 0.6 + 1.96*sqrt(0.6*0.95/repli),
#            alpha = 0.2, fill = "blue") +
#   labs(title = "Slope Estimates of QR = 0.5", x = "Sample Size")
# saveRDS(skew_qr, "skew_qr.rds")
# print(skew_qr)
```

```{r}
power_coverage_skew <- ols_qr_skew %>%
  group_by(Skewness, Sample_Size, Model) %>%
  summarise(
    Power_rate    = mean(Power) * 100,
    Coverage_rate = mean(Coverage) * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

power_skew <- ggplot(power_coverage_skew, aes(x = Sample_Size, y = Power_rate, color = Model)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ Skewness, scales = "free_y") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 95, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", 
           xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = 95 - 1.96 * sqrt(0.05 * 0.95/repli)*100,
           ymax = 95 + 1.96 * sqrt(0.05 * 0.95/repli)*100,
           alpha = 0.1, fill = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Power Percentage of Skewed Data")

coverage_skew <- ggplot(power_coverage_skew, aes(x = Sample_Size, y = Coverage_rate, color = Model)) +
  geom_line() +
  facet_wrap(~ Skewness, scales = "free_y") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 95, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", 
           xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = 95 - 1.96 * sqrt(0.05 * 0.95/repli)*100,
           ymax = 95 + 1.96 * sqrt(0.05 * 0.95/repli)*100,
           alpha = 0.1, fill = "blue") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Coverage Percentage of Skewed Data")

saveRDS(power_skew, "power_skew.rds")
saveRDS(coverage_skew, "coverage_skew.rds")
power_skew
coverage_skew
```

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
set.seed(479)
typeI_results <- list()

for (skew in skewness) {
  cat("\nSkewness =", skew, "\n")
 
  for (i in seq_along(sample_size)) {
    n <- sample_size[i]
   
    reject_ols <- logical(repli)
    reject_qr  <- logical(repli)
   
    for (j in 1:repli) {
      # Generate error
      if (skew == 0) {
        error <- rnorm(n, mean = mean_error, sd = sigma_error)
      } else {
        shape <- 4 / skew^2
        rate <- sqrt(shape) / sigma_error
        error <- rgamma(n, shape = shape, rate = rate)
        error <- error - shape / rate   # center to mean 0
      }
     
      x <- runif(n, 0, 5)
      y <- beta0_original + 0 * x + error
     
      dat <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)
     
      # OLS
      ols_fit <- lm(y ~ x, data = dat)
      p_ols <- summary(ols_fit)$coefficients["x", "Pr(>|t|)"]
      reject_ols[j] <- p_ols < 0.05
     
      qr_fit <- rq(y ~ x, tau = 0.5, data = dat)
      sum_qr <- summary(qr_fit, se = "boot", bsmethod = "xy", R = 200)
      se_qr  <- sum_qr$coefficients["x", "Std. Error"]
      ci_l   <- coef(qr_fit)[2] - 1.96 * se_qr
      ci_u   <- coef(qr_fit)[2] + 1.96 * se_qr
      reject_qr[j] <- (ci_l > 0) || (ci_u < 0)
    }
   
    p_ols <- mean(reject_ols)
    se_ols <- sqrt(p_ols * (1 - p_ols) / repli)
    ci_ols_lower <- p_ols - 1.96 * se_ols
    ci_ols_upper <- p_ols + 1.96 * se_ols
   
    # Calculate Type I error rate + 95% CI for QR
    p_qr <- mean(reject_qr)
    se_qr <- sqrt(p_qr * (1 - p_qr) / repli)
    ci_qr_lower <- p_qr - 1.96 * se_qr
    ci_qr_upper <- p_qr + 1.96 * se_qr
   
    typeI_results <- append(typeI_results, list(
      tibble(
        Skewness      = skew,
        Sample_Size   = n,
        Model         = "OLS",
        TypeI_Error   = p_ols,
        TypeI_Lower   = ci_ols_lower,
        TypeI_Upper   = ci_ols_upper,
        Rejections    = sum(reject_ols)
      )
    ))
    
    typeI_results <- append(typeI_results, list(
      tibble(
        Skewness      = skew,
        Sample_Size   = n,
        Model         = "QR_0.5",
        TypeI_Error   = p_qr,
        TypeI_Lower   = ci_qr_lower,
        TypeI_Upper   = ci_qr_upper,
        Rejections    = sum(reject_qr)
      )
    ))
  }
}

typeI_df <- bind_rows(typeI_results)

# Display nice table
typeI_df %>%
  select(Skewness, Sample_Size, Model, TypeI_Error, TypeI_Lower, TypeI_Upper, Rejections) %>%
  arrange(Skewness, Sample_Size, Model)
```

```{r eval = T, warning = F, message = F}
typei_2models_skew <- ggplot(typeI_df, aes(x = factor(Sample_Size), y = TypeI_Error, color = Model)) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = TypeI_Lower, ymax = TypeI_Upper), width = 0.2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0.05, linetype = "dashed", color = "red", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("rect", 
           xmin = -Inf, xmax = Inf,
           ymin = 0.05 - 1.96 * sqrt(0.05 * 0.95 / repli),
           ymax = 0.05 + 1.96 * sqrt(0.05 * 0.95 / repli),
           alpha = 0.1, fill = "blue") +
  facet_wrap(~ Skewness, labeller = label_both) +
  labs(title = "Type I Error Rate",
       x = "Sample Size",
       y = "Type I Error Rate") +
  theme_minimal()

saveRDS(typei_2models_skew, "typei_2models_skew.rds")
typei_2models_skew
```

```{r}

```
